  • Force Fields : Water
    • Intermolecular Interactions: General Principles
    • Water Models Overview
    • Detailed Water Model Comparison
    • 1. SPC (Simple Point Charge)
    • 2. SPC/E (SPC Extended)
    • 3. TIP3P (Transferable Intermolecular Potential, 3-site)
    • 4. TIP4P (Transferable Intermolecular Potential, 4-site)
    • 5. TIP4P/2005 (TIP4P Refined, 2005)
    • 6. TIP4P/Ice
    • 7. TIP4P/Ew (TIP4P with Ewald summation)
    • 8. SPC/Fw (SPC Flexible Water)
    • 9. q-TIP4P/f (Quantum TIP4P/f)
    • Comparative Summary Table
    • Hydrogen Bonding in Water Models
    • Choosing a Water Model for Your Simulation
    • Extensions and Modern Developments
    • References and Further Reading

Math

  • Functions and Domains
    • Definition
    • Example
    • Notes
  • Limits
    • Why limits matter
    • Informal idea
    • Formal definition (epsilon-delta)
    • One-sided limits
    • Basic properties of limits
    • Order theorem and squeeze theorem
    • Limits and continuity
    • Infinite limits
    • Limits at infinity
    • Remarkable limits
    • Indeterminate forms
    • Most common techniques
    • Worked examples
      • Example 1: direct substitution
      • Example 2: factor and cancel
      • Example 3: conjugate
      • Example 4: one-sided limits
      • Example 5: squeeze theorem
      • Example 6: remarkable trigonometric limit
      • Example 7: limit at infinity of a rational function
      • Example 8: infinite limit
    • Summary
  • Integrals
    • What is an integral?
    • Most common methods
    • Integration by parts (IDV-VDI)
      • Worked examples (3)
        • Example 1: \(\int x e^x\,dx\)
        • Example 2: \(\int x\ln x\,dx\)
        • Example 3: \(\int e^x\sin x\,dx\)
    • Substitution
      • Worked examples (3)
        • Example 1: \(\int 2x\cos(x^2)\,dx\)
        • Example 2: \(\int \frac{x}{x^2+1}\,dx\)
        • Example 3: \(\int (3x+1)^5\,dx\)
    • Trigonometric substitution
      • Worked examples (3)
        • Example 1: \(\int \frac{dx}{\sqrt{9-x^2}}\)
        • Example 2: \(\int \frac{dx}{x^2+4}\)
        • Example 3: \(\int \frac{dx}{\sqrt{x^2-9}}\)
    • Polynomial coefficients (partial fractions)
      • Worked examples (3)
        • Example 1: \(\int \frac{3x+5}{x^2+3x+2}\,dx\)
        • Example 2: \(\int \frac{5x+3}{x(x+1)^2}\,dx\)
        • Example 3: \(\int \frac{2x^2+3x+4}{x(x^2+1)}\,dx\)
  • Stokes’ Theorem
    • What the theorem says
    • Geometric meaning
    • Orientation rule
    • When to use it
    • Practical checklist
    • Worked examples
      • Example 1: closed line integral on a circle
      • Example 2: triangle in an oblique plane
      • Example 3: use Stokes in the reverse direction
    • Summary
  • Residue Theorem
    • Summary (Arfken)
    • Intuition (why it works)
    • Residue formulas
    • Equivalent identities (handy in practice)
    • Fast residue methods (beyond the limit formula)
    • Practical checklist
    • Notes on contour choices
    • Methodology
    • Worked examples
      • Example 1: \(\int_{-\infty}^{\infty} \frac{dx}{x^2+a^2}\)
      • Example 2: \(\int_0^{\infty} \frac{\cos(bx)}{x^2+a^2}\,dx\)
      • Example 3: \(\int_0^{2\pi} \frac{d\theta}{a+b\cos\theta}\)
      • Example 4: \(\int_0^{2\pi} \cos^{2n}(x)\,dx\)
      • Example 5 (keyhole/branch cut): \(\int_0^{\infty} \frac{x^{\alpha-1}}{1+x}\,dx\)
      • Example 6 (pole on the integration path): \(\mathrm{PV}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty} \frac{\sin x}{x}\,dx\)
    • Additional examples to practice
